mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.5%, tight labor market conditions trigger wage inflation pressures that compress corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where housing starts rise while industrial production stagnates signals a reallocation of capital toward residential construction, crowding out corporate investment and depressing equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a Rank IC > 0.04 when tested against a single-proxy baseline over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, rising consumer credit balances reflect unsustainable debt accumulation that precedes consumption pullbacks, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when the trigger condition is met, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand, which directly boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the rolling 1-year Rank IC consistently falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index exceeds its 12-month moving average by more than 2%, supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints emerge, leading to margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when the threshold is breached, or the signal's out-of-sample Sharpe ratio drops below 0.1 over a 3-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in payroll employment and the federal funds rate signals that the labor market is overheating while the central bank is actively tightening, creating a high probability of policy-induced equity drawdowns over a one-quarter horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in predictive accuracy, or forward returns remain positive in over 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery phase, rapid growth in housing starts combined with falling unemployment indicates a broad-based economic rebound that fuels equity risk appetite and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts negative returns in more than 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 2-year window.
